name: Settings Database Diagnostics
summary: Developer-facing database diagnostics screen that runs a MediatR command, checks MySQL connectivity, validates expected settings tables and stored procedures, and probes core settings DAOs.

userActions: Run all diagnostics from one command
Review connection state and response time
See which expected tables are missing or found
See which expected stored procedures are missing or found
See whether the core settings DAOs are operational
preserveRules: Keep the command-based flow from ViewModel to MediatR handler intact
Keep the diagnostics screen focused on validation and reporting rather than mutation
acceptanceCriteria: Run Tests still invokes the command and populates summary, tables, stored procedures, and DAO results
The diagnostics remain read-only and do not write to settings data
Metadata reflects the actual expected tables, procedures, and DAO checks described by the handler
validationHints: Confirm Run Tests still updates connection state and validation counts
Confirm table, stored procedure, and DAO result lists still populate the correct pivot tabs
Confirm the diagnostics still probe the expected settings tables and stored procedures
sensitiveDataRules: Do not log or expose raw connection strings in the diagnostics UI
Keep database diagnostics limited to health and metadata checks
